design_note: v2 redesign (Stage 3.5). The original problem_003 v1 measured 0% fail rate against Qwen3.5 because the docstring contract on sanitize_thread plus the load-bearing test name test_sanitize_thread_excludes_none_entries telegraphed the multi-site exclusion semantics directly to the model. v2 hides the contract surface: function names are neutral generics (process / fold / shorten / clean — no thread / sanitize / exclude verbs), docstrings carry NO behavioral contract beyond what the input/output types describe, and the load-bearing semantic decision (None entries are dropped from the collected output, NOT mapped to empty strings) is documented ONLY through test input/output examples. The model has to read test cases like collect([item, None, item]) -> [str, str] and INFER that None gets filtered before reaching process(). Naive leaf-only None-safety still produces [str, '', str] and fails the inference test. Multi-site coordination (leaf + aggregator) remains required; the difference vs v1 is that the LLM cannot pattern-match function names or docstrings to find the trap.
